target.name: model-pyramid skill — scripts/decide.mjs CLI + exported checkAssignment/mapToRuntime + SKILL.md/runtime-mapping.md contract

context_sources: user-provided: build-pipeline handoff (documented CLI entry, checkAssignment surface, declared input domain, doc-claim scope)
self-researched: SKILL.md rule card + references/runtime-mapping.md (the requirement artifacts; implementation source, evals/, .skill-engineer/ withheld)
in_scope: decision-rule correctness of the decide.mjs CLI (R1/R2/R2b/R3, precedence, floor, one-knob, layers)
input-domain robustness of the documented CLI entry (malformed/boundary/unicode/type-confusion inputs)
doc-claim coverage: SKILL.md rule card + references/runtime-mapping.md vs implementation behavior
out_of_scope: trigger/activation behavior of the skill description (agent-level, not CLI-testable in this battery)
prose framing discipline outside machine-checkable outputs (e.g. never-pitch-as-cost-reduction in conversation)
evals/ and .skill-engineer/ content (independence firewall — never opened)
summary.context_digest: Fresh round-1 battery vs model-pyramid v0.1.0. Requirement = SKILL.md + references/runtime-mapping.md only. Invoked: documented CLI (node scripts/decide.mjs '<json>'), exported checkAssignment(session, assignment), exported mapToRuntime(effort, runtime), decideBatch/decide via module import. Implementation source never read; error output observed as behavior only. 74 probe cases: 62 domain-derived edges + 12 doc-claim coverage checks.

target.name: checkAssignment(session, assignment) — exported audit function of scripts/decide.mjs

records.invariant: SKILL.md ('Verify the pyramid shape'): a needs-justification flag is owed for 'any haiku-tier pick from a HIGHER-TIER session'. A small-tier session assigning its own small tier — including the identity assignment (keep/keep) — is not a pick from a higher-tier session and must not be flagged; decide() itself implements exactly this scoping.

baseline.metric: checkAssignment flags for an identity (keep/keep) assignment
baseline.value: [] — the identity assignment audits clean at opus-tier: checkAssignment({tier:'opus-tier',effort:'max'},{tier:'opus-tier',effort:'max'}) === []; and the documented flag case stays correct: sonnet-tier session -> haiku-tier assignment === ['needs-justification']
baseline.observed_via: node module import of the exported function (real seam, no mocks)

records.non_tautology_check: The probe discriminates a correct implementation: decide() itself, run in the same haiku-tier session (e.g. decide({exploratory:true},{tier:'haiku-tier',effort:'high'}) -> tier 'small', justification_required:false, report without [needs-justification]), returns the docs-conformant answer for the identical (session, assignment) pair — so an implementation that scoped the flag to 'from a higher-tier session' passes this probe, and checkAssignment fails it. Controls: opus identity -> [] (flag CAN be absent); sonnet->haiku -> ['needs-justification'] (flag CAN fire where owed).

independence_attestation.derived_expected_from: SKILL.md 'Verify the pyramid shape' ('any haiku-tier pick from a higher-tier session needs an explicit one-line justification') + 'Report & overrides' flag semantics

evidence.differential_pair: decide({},{tier:'haiku-tier',effort:'high'}) -> {tier:'small',effort:'medium',justification_required:false} BUT checkAssignment({tier:'haiku-tier',effort:'high'},{tier:'small',effort:'medium'}) -> ['needs-justification'] — decide()'s own emitted assignment fails decide.mjs's own audit for every small-tier session
evidence.blast_radius: every assignment audited in a small/haiku-tier session is false-positively flagged, including the R1 keep/keep and every R2/R3 output of decide() itself

target.name: decide.mjs CLI session-intake (tier vs effort enum normalization)

records.attack_scope: input-domain robustness of the documented CLI entry (malformed/boundary/unicode/type-confusion inputs)
records.invariant: The two session enum fields are read under one intake discipline: a case-variant of a valid documented notch is either normalized (as the tier field provably does: 'OPUS-TIER'/'Opus-Tier' -> frontier) or rejected loudly (as unknown tiers are: exit 1). An explicitly stated session effort must never be silently replaced with a different notch, and the [assumed-high] flag is documented for 'effort not introspectable' (Preflight 2), not for a stated value.

baseline.metric: R1 (peer) decision for a max-effort session
baseline.value: lowercase control: {"session":{"tier":"opus-tier","effort":"max"}} -> effort 'max', assumed_effort:false, report 'rule=peer tier=frontier effort=max'; and the tier field under the SAME case perturbation is accepted ('OPUS-TIER' -> frontier, no error)

records.non_tautology_check: A consistent implementation passes this probe two ways: (a) normalize effort case exactly as it already normalizes tier case ('MAX' -> max), or (b) reject unknown effort strings exactly as it rejects unknown tiers (loud exit 1). The shipped code demonstrably contains case normalization (tier path), so the probe is a real path a correct impl would pass — it fails only because R1 'keep' then keeps a silently DOWNGRADED effort (max -> high) for a user who explicitly stated MAX, mislabelled with the not-introspectable flag. Same class re-fires for out-of-vocabulary-but-unambiguous values: effort 'xhigh' (the documented Codex equivalent of max) -> 'high [assumed-high]'.

independence_attestation.derived_expected_from: SKILL.md Preflight 1–2 (effort ladder low<medium<high<max; assume-high is scoped to 'effort not introspectable') + references/runtime-mapping.md (codex 'xhigh' ≡ max correspondence) + the product's own tier-intake behavior as differential reference

evidence.asymmetry: unknown tier 'gpt-5' -> loud error exit 1; unknown effort 'ultra'/'MAX'/'xhigh' -> silent assumed-high exit 0
evidence.impact: an R1/R2b 'keep' decision emits one notch below the session's stated effort; the [assumed-high] flag misreports a stated value as not-introspectable

needs_judgment.observed: tasks:[null] -> internal TypeError text 'Cannot read properties of null (reading exploratory)' exit 1; tasks:['x'] and tasks:[42] -> silently accepted as R3 default decisions (exit 0)
needs_judgment.expected: one consistent behavior for the non-object-task class (uniform validation error, or uniform default)
needs_judgment.note: Three different behaviors for one malformed-input class. The contract never promises non-object task handling, so per anti-vacuity this is not a confirmed finding; the internal-TypeError message is below the CLI's own graceful-error baseline ('unknown capability tier ... (use ...)') but the rejection outcome itself is defensible.

needs_judgment.observed: checkAssignment maps a MISSING assignment effort and an UNKNOWN effort ('turbo') both to the flag 'below-floor', while unknown tiers get a dedicated 'unknown-tier' flag
needs_judgment.expected: an unknown/missing effort labelled as unknown (cf. 'unknown-tier'), not asserted to sit below the floor
needs_judgment.note: Docs define below-floor as effort below medium; 'turbo'/absent is not provably below medium. Fail-closed flagging is defensible advisory behavior, but the label is a misdiagnosis. Docs are silent -> flag, not a proven break.

needs_judgment.observed: report lines use rule ids 'peer'/'search'/'search-tier-drop'/'default'/'override'; the SKILL.md rule table names the rules R1/R2/R2b/R3
needs_judgment.expected: rule=<id> resolvable to the documented rule table without guessing (e.g. rule=R2b or a documented id mapping)
needs_judgment.note: The documented format 'rule=<id> tier=<tier> effort=<notch>' is satisfied shape-wise and the names map 1:1, but nothing in SKILL.md documents the emitted id vocabulary. Naming mismatch, docs ambiguous -> flag.

needs_judgment.observed: documented CLI accepts input only as one argv string (stdin prints usage, exit 2); inputs beyond ARG_MAX (~1MB, roughly >40k tasks) cannot be passed through the documented entry point. 5000 tasks complete in ~40ms; empty tasks -> []; idempotent (byte-identical reruns)
needs_judgment.expected: n/a — scale behavior within the reachable domain is healthy
needs_judgment.note: Not promised, and decideBatch() covers programmatic large batches; recorded so the 'huge tasks array' scope item is visibly adjudicated rather than skipped.

needs_judgment.observed: the override knob is the task key 'user_override' (applied verbatim + floor caveat + report line, exactly as SKILL.md promises); a task key 'override' (and task-level tier/effort) is SILENTLY ignored and falls through to rule=default
needs_judgment.expected: the override input shape documented somewhere in SKILL.md/runtime-mapping.md, or unknown-looking override keys warned about
needs_judgment.note: The user-override-wins doc claim IS honored for the right key (verbatim apply, '[caveat: user override low sits below the pyramid's medium floor ...]'). The break-shaped part — a user guessing 'override' gets a silent R3 default — cannot be proven against any documented input contract because the docs never name the key. Doc-coverage gap -> flag.

out_of_scope.note: compatibility claim 'node >= 18' untested — battery ran on node v24.16.0 only; no node-18 runtime available in this environment

SKILL.md metrics line references evals/run_all.mjs and evals/cases/trigger-cases.json — path existence confirmed via directory listing only; contents deliberately never opened (independence firewall), so the 'decision accuracy target 100%' and 'activation precision >= 0.9' metric claims are unadjudicated by this battery

'at most 3 illustrative model names skill-wide' verified by mechanical pattern-grep over SKILL.md + references/ + scripts/ (exactly 3: claude-opus-4-5, claude-sonnet-4-5, claude-haiku-4-5, all in runtime-mapping.md as documented); evals/ and .skill-* excluded from the sweep by the independence constraint, so 'skill-wide' is confirmed for the shipped contract surface only
